name: Supernova 1987A
description: A supernova in the Large Magellanic Cloud, a nearby dwarf galaxy. It was the closest observed supernova since Kepler's Supernova in 1604.

name: Total Solar Eclipse 2024
description: A total solar eclipse that was visible across a band of North America, from Mexico, through the United States, and into Canada.

name: Perseids Meteor Shower 2025
description: The Perseids are a prolific meteor shower associated with the comet Swift–Tuttle. The peak of the shower is expected around mid-August.

name: Simulated Alpha Centauri Flare
description: A simulated stellar flare event on Proxima Centauri, the closest known star to the Sun. This event showcases potential high-energy phenomena.

name: Comet Halley Return (Simulated Observation)
description: A simulated early observation of Comet Halley's next perihelion passage. Halley's Comet is a short-period comet visible from Earth every 75–79 years.

name: Andromeda-Milky Way Collision Preview
description: A simulated artistic representation of the future collision between the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies, billions of years from now.
